problem_addressed: The need for a comprehensive benchmark to evaluate LLMs as agents in interactive environments, revealing performance gaps between commercial and open-source models.
core_claim: AGENTBENCH provides a standardized, multi-dimensional benchmark that holistically assesses LLM agent capabilities, exposing critical weaknesses in reasoning and instruction following.
key_contributions: Introduction of AGENTBENCH with 8 distinct interactive environments (OS, Database, Knowledge, Graph, Digital Card Game, Lateral Thinking Puzzle, House-keeping, Web Browsing/Shopping)
Extensive evaluation of 29 API-based and open-source LLMs, revealing significant performance disparities (commercial avg: 4.01 vs OSS avg: 0.51)
Identification of failure reasons: poor long-term reasoning, decision-making, and instruction following abilities
novelty: Unlike prior benchmarks focusing on single tasks, AGENTBENCH uniquely combines multiple interactive environments to holistically evaluate LLM agent capabilities with real-world applicability.
method_summary: The method constructs 8 interactive environments simulating real-world tasks, evaluates LLMs through task completion metrics, and analyzes failure patterns across reasoning, decision-making, and instruction-following dimensions.
method_components: Environment design with specific interaction protocols (e.g., OS command execution, database queries, web navigation)
Evaluation framework measuring task success rates and error categorization
Comparative analysis across 29 models including API-based (GPT-4, Claude-3) and open-source (Llama-2-70B, Vicuna-13B)
key_innovation: The multi-environment benchmark architecture that simultaneously tests diverse agent capabilities (reasoning, decision-making, instruction following) in interactive settings, revealing systemic weaknesses.
assumptions: LLMs can function as autonomous agents in structured environments
Performance in benchmark environments correlates with real-world agent utility
Task completion rates are valid proxies for agent competence
theoretical_grounding: Empirical evaluation framework without formal proofs; relies on statistical analysis of task performance across environments and model scales.
headline_result: gpt-3.5-turbo achieves 0.92 on UPDATE operations in re-annotated data
improvement_over_baseline: gpt-4 improves on UPDATE operations from 0.32 to 0.66 in re-annotated data
best_result: gpt-3.5-turbo achieves 0.92 on UPDATE operations in re-annotated data
title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
